title: Binance Launches 135M Token Airdrop for USD1 Holders
subtitle: A four-week distribution of World Liberty Financial tokens to holders of the Trump-linked USD1 stablecoin — the second such campaign this quarter.
content_html:
Binance, the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume, launched a new airdrop campaign on March 20, 2026, distributing 135 million World Liberty Financial (WLFI) tokens to users who hold the platform's USD1 stablecoin. The four-week promotion runs through April 17, with 33.75 million WLFI tokens released each Friday to eligible holders. It marks the second consecutive airdrop campaign Binance has run for World Liberty Financial, a decentralized finance project linked to the Trump family.

Overview

World Liberty Financial (WLFI) is a DeFi lending and staking protocol that launched in late 2024 with prominent backing from Donald Trump, his sons Eric and Donald Jr., and son-in-law Jared Kushner. The project issues two tokens: WLFI, a governance token, and USD1, a dollar-pegged stablecoin minted on Ethereum and BNB Chain.

The Binance airdrop campaign is designed to reward holders of USD1 on the Binance platform with governance tokens in the underlying project. Participants do not need to actively trade or stake; holding a qualifying balance of USD1 on Binance at the time of each Friday snapshot makes a user eligible for that week's WLFI distribution.

Airdrop Structure

The Round 2 campaign distributes a total of 135,000,000 WLFI tokens over four weekly tranches:

Distribution Date WLFI Tokens Cumulative Total
Friday, March 21, 2026 33,750,000 WLFI 33,750,000
Friday, March 28, 2026 33,750,000 WLFI 67,500,000
Friday, April 4, 2026 33,750,000 WLFI 101,250,000
Friday, April 17, 2026 33,750,000 WLFI 135,000,000

Eligibility is determined by a snapshot of USD1 balances on the Binance exchange at an unannounced cutoff time each week. The amount of WLFI allocated to each user is proportional to their USD1 holding relative to the total pool of participating balances. Binance has not published a minimum holding threshold.

What Is World Liberty Financial

World Liberty Financial is a decentralized finance protocol built on Ethereum and BNB Chain. The project describes itself as a DeFi lending platform that allows users to borrow against crypto collateral and earn yield on stablecoins. Its governance token, WLFI, enables holders to vote on protocol parameters and future development decisions.

The project attracted major public attention due to its association with Donald Trump and members of the Trump family. Trump holds a reported 75% stake in the entity commercializing WLFI, according to financial disclosures filed during the 2024 presidential campaign. His sons Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. appeared at the project's public launch events.

WLFI completed a token sale in late 2024 that raised over $300 million, with buyers including Tron founder Justin Sun, who invested approximately $75 million. The WLFI token has remained largely non-liquid since launch, with the project restricting transfers on governance tokens outside of its internal ecosystem.

USD1 — The Trump Stablecoin

USD1 is the stablecoin issued by World Liberty Financial, launched in early 2025. It is pegged 1:1 to the US dollar and backed by a reserve of short-term US government treasuries and cash equivalents, audited by third-party custodians.

USD1 is issued on both the Ethereum and BNB Chain networks. Binance's integration of USD1 as a listed spot asset — and its decision to run back-to-back airdrop promotions tied to USD1 holding — has served as a key distribution mechanism for the token, which launched without the organic exchange integration history of competitors like USDC or USDT.

Critics, including several US senators on the Senate Banking Committee, have raised concerns about the stablecoin's conflict-of-interest implications given Donald Trump's role in crafting federal stablecoin legislation simultaneously with his financial stake in USD1. The GENIUS Act, a Senate stablecoin regulatory bill, was under active debate at the time of this campaign's launch.

Previous Binance Campaign

The March 20 campaign is the second airdrop Binance has run tied to World Liberty Financial. The first campaign began on February 20, 2026, and distributed a larger pool of 235 million WLFI tokens over the month preceding the current round.

Round 1 ended on approximately March 19–20, meaning the two campaigns run nearly consecutively with no gap between distribution windows. The combined total across both campaigns amounts to 370 million WLFI tokens distributed exclusively through Binance's USD1-holding incentive program within a two-month window.

Significance and Scrutiny

The consecutive Binance airdrop campaigns have drawn attention for several reasons:

  • Scale of distribution: 370 million WLFI tokens distributed through a single exchange partner in under 60 days is an unusually concentrated promotional strategy for a project claiming DeFi decentralization.
  • Lock-in incentive: The campaigns encourage users to hold USD1 on Binance — a centralized custodian — rather than self-custody or distribute the stablecoin on-chain, contradicting typical DeFi user behavior.
  • Regulatory optics: With Trump's administration simultaneously advancing stablecoin legislation that could benefit USD1, the airdrop campaigns have been cited by ethics watchdogs as a potential channel for presidential financial self- dealing through crypto incentivization.
  • Binance relationship: Binance CEO Richard Teng met with Trump-era treasury and commerce officials in early 2025. The exchange, which settled a $4.3 billion DOJ case in 2023 and operates under a compliance monitor, has sought regulatory rehabilitation under the Trump administration's more crypto-friendly posture.

Neither Binance nor World Liberty Financial has publicly disclosed the commercial terms of the airdrop partnership, including whether Binance is compensated in WLFI tokens or other consideration for hosting the distribution.

References

  1. Binance announcement — Binance USD1 + WLFI airdrop campaign, March 20, 2026.
  2. World Liberty Financial WLFI tokenomics disclosure, 2024 launch documents.
  3. U.S. Senate Banking Committee letter on USD1 conflict-of-interest concerns, February 2026.
  4. Trump financial disclosure filings, FEC, 2024 presidential campaign.
  5. Binance DOJ settlement documents, November 2023 — $4.3 billion penalty, compliance monitor appointment.
